table_semantics.justification: Full table structure with <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <tfoot>. All header cells use correct scope: plan names have scope='col', feature names have scope='row'. Table used only for tabular data, not layout.

element_choice.justification:
CTA links correctly use elements (they navigate to pages), prices are in /

not headings, plan cards use

, lists use
    . No div/span soup for interactive or semantic content.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> for the page title, two <h2> headings for major sections, and <h3> for plan names within those sections. No skipped levels, prices are not headings, 'Most Popular' badge is not a heading.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used sparingly and correctly: aria-labelledby on sections and articles, aria-label on CTAs to disambiguate repeated text, aria-current='page' on active nav link, visually-hidden span for 'Most Popular' in the table header. No redundant ARIA roles. The aria-label on the <strong> 'Most Popular' badge is slightly unusual but the visually-hidden pattern in the table is the cleaner and more important instance.

landmark_structure.justification: <header>, <main>, <footer> used correctly. Two <nav> elements both labelled with aria-label. <section> elements use aria-labelledby. Skip link present. <main> wraps only page-specific content.

content_realism.justification: Realistic plan descriptions, plausible company name, actual feature data matches the prompt exactly. No lorem ipsum or placeholder text.

list_semantics.justification: Plan cards presented in a <ul>, navigation items in <ul> within <nav> (both primary and footer). Appropriate list types used throughout.

expectations_met: Plan names are <th scope='col'> in the header row
Feature names are <th scope='row'> in each row
Yes/No values conveyed as text (not icons or colour only)
'Most Popular' badge is accessible via visually-hidden span in table header and aria-label on the badge element
CTA buttons use <a> elements (navigating to pages)
Plan names use <h3> under <h2> section headings
Boolean features use plain text 'Yes'/'No' with no icon-only representation
additional_observations: Excellent use of aria-label on CTA links to disambiguate repeated 'Start Free Trial' text for screen reader users
tabindex='-1' on <main> is a good practice to allow the skip link to focus programmatically
The <tfoot> row repeating prices is a nice usability touch for long comparison tables
